inputs.label: Ingredient Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Standard cost accounting for raw materials; based on APICS dictionary and typical manufacturing cost breakdown.

inputs.label: Ingredient Quantity
inputs.businessContext: Recipe bill of materials; quantity per batch or per unit output.

inputs.label: Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Direct labor cost; includes wages and benefits; standard in cost accounting.

inputs.label: Labor Hours per Batch
inputs.businessContext: Time studies or standard labor hours; based on Maynard's Industrial Engineering Handbook.

inputs.label: Overhead Rate
inputs.businessContext: Manufacturing overhead as percentage of direct costs; typical in cost accounting.

inputs.label: Batch Size
inputs.businessContext: Number of units produced per batch; affects cost per unit.

inputs.label: Waste Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Material waste or yield loss; based on Six Sigma DMAIC and lean manufacturing principles.

inputs.label: Energy Cost per kWh
inputs.businessContext: Energy cost for processing; ISO 50001 energy management standard.

inputs.label: Energy Consumption (kWh)
inputs.businessContext: Energy used per batch; from energy audits or equipment specs.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Subjective confidence in input data accuracy; affects adjusted cost output.

thresholds.wastePercentage > 10: High waste percentage detected; consider process improvement.
thresholds.overheadRate > 50: Overhead rate exceeds typical range; review indirect costs.
thresholds.energyCostPerKwh > 0.15: Energy cost above benchmark; consider energy efficiency measures.

suggestedActions: Reduce waste through process optimization (Lean/Six Sigma).
Negotiate lower ingredient prices or find alternative suppliers.
Improve labor productivity via training or automation.
Review overhead allocation and reduce non-value-added activities.
Implement energy management system (ISO 50001) to lower energy costs.
